entities.name: HP Action for Repairs and Services
entities.description: Tenants may start a lawsuit against their landlords based on the landlord's failure to make repairs or provide essential services.

entities.name: Rent Reduction for Decreased Services
entities.description: Tenants in rent-stabilized or rent-controlled apartments can file for a rent reduction based on decreased services.

entities.name: Breach of Warranty of Habitability
entities.description: Tenants have a right to withhold rent or seek a rent abatement if they can document not getting repairs or essential services for a period of time.

entities.name: Retaliatory Eviction
entities.description: If a landlord refuses to renew a lease or gives a significant rent increase within a year of a repair request, it could be considered retaliation.

entities.name: Written Letter to Landlord
entities.description: A letter listing all problems and history of previous attempts to get them solved, sent by certified mail, return receipt requested.
entities.source_quote: You must send the letter by certified mail, return receipt requested, and keep a copy of the letter, along with proof of mailing and the receipt

entities.name: Tenants' Inspection Checklist
entities.description: A checklist to document all needed repairs in the apartment and common areas.
entities.source_quote: you may want to download and print our Tenants' Inspection Checklist... and use it as a guide so that you don't leave anything out

entities.name: HPD Violation List
entities.description: Printout of violations for the building and apartment from HPD's website.
entities.source_quote: Print out the list of violations for your building and apartment from HPD's website: nyc.gov/hpd

entities.name: Photos of Violations
entities.description: New pictures of any violations that can be seen.
entities.source_quote: Take new pictures of any violations that can be seen

entities.name: Proof of Mailings
entities.description: Proof of mailing letters to the landlord from the post office.
entities.source_quote: proof of your efforts to get your landlord to comply (such as letters to your landlord with proof of mailing from the post office)

entities.name: Violation Reports from City Agencies
entities.description: Reports from HPD, DOB, or other agencies documenting conditions.

entities.name: Heat Logs
entities.description: Records documenting lack of heat or hot water.

entities.name: HCR Form RA-81 or RA-84
entities.description: Application forms for rent reduction based on decreased services.
entities.source_quote: Individual tenants should use HCR's form RA-81 'Application For A Rent Reduction Based Upon Decreased Service(s) – Individual Apartment' and tenant groups should use form RA-84 'Application For A Rent Reduction Based Upon Decreased Building-Wide Service(s)'

entities.name: File Complaint with City Agencies via 311
entities.description: Process for requesting inspections and enforcement from city housing, building, and health departments.

steps: Call 311 (or 212-639-9675 from outside NYC)
Request inspection from appropriate agency (HPD, DOB, DOH, DEP)
Get complaint number
Follow up with local elected officials if needed

entities.name: File HP Action in Housing Court
entities.description: Process for suing landlord in Housing Court for repairs and services.

steps: Go to Housing Court in your borough
Ask for paperwork to start HP Action
Get help filling forms at clerks' windows or Court Help Center
File forms with clerk and pay fee (or request waiver)
Get court date and inspection date
Serve landlord and city with papers
Be home on inspection date
Return to court on specified date

entities.name: File Rent Reduction Complaint with HCR
entities.description: Process for rent-regulated tenants to apply for rent reduction due to decreased services.

steps: Write letter to landlord complaining about problem
Send letter by certified mail, return receipt requested
Wait 10-60 days after sending letter
File HCR Form RA-81 (individual) or RA-84 (group)
Submit copy of violation report if no heat/hot water

entities.name: File Order to Show Cause
entities.description: Process to return to court when landlord ignores court orders.

steps: Return to court
File Order to Show Cause
Ask for new court date

entities.name: Court Order to Correct Violations
entities.description: Landlord signs court order to correct violations listed on inspection report with arrangement for access dates.

entities.name: HPD Ordered Repairs
entities.description: In extreme cases, judge can order HPD to do repairs and bill landlord.

entities.name: Rent Reduction and Freeze
entities.description: HCR orders rent reduction and rent freeze for rent-regulated apartments until landlord fixes problem.

entities.name: Rent Abatement
entities.description: Court orders reduction in amount of rent owed due to lack of repairs or essential services.

entities.name: New Lease for Retaliation
entities.description: Courts can give tenant new lease for a year if landlord retaliates for repair requests.

entities.name: Housing Maintenance Laws
entities.description: Laws requiring landlords to maintain essential services and make prompt repairs.

entities.name: Rent Stabilization Law
entities.description: Laws governing rent-stabilized apartments including provisions for rent reductions for decreased services.

entities.name: Rent Control Law
entities.description: Laws governing rent-controlled apartments including provisions for rent reductions for decreased services.

section_b.chunk_text_preview: Getting Repairs - Met Council on Housing Skip to content The Basics of Getting RepairsThe laws and regulations of New York require that your landlord maintain essential services in your building and apartment and that they make prompt repairs.
